title: Graphs and Functions
description: Plotting points and functions on the coordinate plane, straight-line graphs y = mx + c, gradient between two points, and reading quadratic graphs from a table of values.
objectives: Plot and read coordinates on the Cartesian plane and interpret function notation f(x) as 'substitute x into the rule'
Identify the gradient m and y-intercept c in y = mx + c and describe how changing each one changes the line
Calculate the gradient of the line through two given points using m = (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1)
Draw up a table of values and plot a quadratic graph y = x^2 + bx + c
Read off the approximate turning point and roots of a plotted quadratic graph
Recognise, from its equation, whether a graph will be a straight line or a parabola
